Motion To Vacate Order Of Dismissal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Plaintiff Juliana Lourenco Lopes Demasi's motion to vacate dismissal is DENIED.

This action was dismissed by the Honorable Rochelle East, the bench officer presiding in Department 610, on January 13, 2026, after Plaintiff failed to appear or otherwise respond to an Order to Show Cause regarding her failure to properly serve the defendants and prosecute her case. Trial courts have the authority to "dismiss sua sponte for disobedience or lack of prosecution." (Lyons v. Wickhorst (1986) 42 Cal.3d 911, 922.) Plaintiff has provided no material evidence or persuasive argument demonstrating the order dismissing the action was improper. Indeed, this court is quite familiar with the record and aware Plaintiff has not served any defendants or taken meaningful action to prosecute her case. This court has repeatedly advised Plaintiff that she must serve defendants and file proper proof of service that she has done so. There is no evidence that she has taken any steps toward either end or even that she intends to do so. As such, the court denies her motion to vacate the dismissal.

Plaintiff points to physical and cognitive conditions that make personal appearances difficult. Plaintiff, however, failed to show in writing that she served even a single defendant and she failed to respond to an OSC, which can also be accomplished by a writing. Service, itself, of course, does not require a personal appearance. The motion is without a factual or legal basis. It, thus, must be denied.

Plaintiff is admonished that she was required to file a proof of service of the moving papers (California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1300), identify the hearing time and place and motion grounds within the first paragraph of her notice of motion (CRC 3.1110(a)-(b)), notice her motion with at least 16 court days' notice (Code Civ. Proc. 1005), and file a memorandum of points and authorities not exceeding 15 pages (CRC 3.1113(d)-(e)). The court has advised and admonished Plaintiff regarding these and similar statutory and rule violations several times in the past. The court does so once again.

To the extent Plaintiff seeks different or additional relief, the motion is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion For Medical Necessity Of Umbilical Cord Blood Treatment To Injuries And Implementation Of Technology At Ucla
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF JULIANA LOPES DEMASI's Motion For Medical Necessity Of Umbilical Cord Blood Treatment To Injuries And Implementation Of Technology At Ucla.

Plaintiff Juliana Lourenco Lopes Demasi's Motion For Medical Necessity Of Umbilical Cord Blood Treatment To Injuries And Implementation Of Technology At UCLA is DENIED.

Plaintiff moves under Civil Code section 1431.2 "and applicable tort principles." Section 1431.2 provides principally as follows: "In any action for personal injury, property damage, or wrongful death, based upon principles of comparative fault, the liability of each defendant for non-economic damages shall be several only and shall not be joint. Each defendant shall be liable only for the amount of non-economic damages allocated to that defendant in direct proportion to that defendant's percentage of fault, and a separate judgment shall be rendered against that defendant for that amount." (See Civil Code section 1431.2(a).)

Section 1431.2 does not authorize an interlocutory award of substantial relief, let alone such an award before Plaintiff has demonstrated that the parties who would be obligated under the order have even been served with the operative pleading and summons. Plaintiff has not identified-and the court is not aware of-any tort principles that would support such relief at this time.

The motion fails.

Specially-appearing Defendants are asked to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

A proposed order is submitted prior to the heearing. Order is signed.

Alexandra C. Seibert, Esq. HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP (415) 288-9800 acs@hassard.com Attorney for Defendant SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS dba CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER (erroneously sued herein as CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER VAN NESS CAMPUS)
No other appearances

Hearing is held. Discussed, not ARGUED. After discussion, The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIANA LOPES DEMASI'S Motion For Default Judgment Against The Seven Defendants.

Plaintiff Juliana Lourenco Demasi's Motion for Default Judgment is DENIED.

The court construes the motion as a motion for entry of default. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 585-587.) Default judgment requires that a party first obtain default(s), which is usually executed by the clerk, then pursue a default judgment in Department 505.

The court is without jurisdiction over some or all of the claims because Plaintiff has filed notices of appeal and the appellate court has not yet issued a remittitur.

The court notes it is without jurisdiction over the Defendant Regents and Defendant Golden Gate Obstetrics & Gynecology, Inc. The court is definitely without jurisdiction at least as to the Sutter Defendants, given Plaintiff's notice s of appeal. The motion is not properly noticed at all, given Plaintiff failed to file a Motion and Notice of Motion. The motion is not properly noticed under Code of Civil Procedure section 1005 and the rules for adding time based on the manner of service of the moving papers. Plaintiff failed timely to file proof of service of her papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300.) Plaintiff has not carried her burden to secure a default against any Defendant. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 585-587.)

Any request to for an order finding Plaintiff vexatious must be presented in a properly noticed motion.

Orer is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Motion To Compel Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-06-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF JULIANA LOPES DEMASI'S Motion To Compel Discovery.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. It appears to the Court that Plaintiff has filed multiple overlapping discovery motions plus several reiterations thereof as follows:
On June 5, 2024, Plaintiff filed a "Motion to Compel Discovery" addressed to multiple parties (including California Pacific Medical Center) and numerous non-parties with a signature date of "06/05/2024" and a hearing date of June 28, 2024. On June 6 and 11, 2024, Plaintiff filed identical copies of said "Motion to Compel Discovery". On June 18, 2024, Plaintiff filed another copy of said motion under the altered title "Motion to Compel Discovery All".

On June 10, 2024, Plaintiff filed another "Motion to Compel Discovery", this one addressed only to California Pacific Medical Center with a signature date of "10/05/2024" and hearing date of June 28, 2024 and seeking the same relief as the motion filed on June 5, 2024. On June 12, 2024, Plaintiff filed another copy of the same motion. On June 18, 2024, Plaintiff filed yet another copy under the altered title "Motion to Compel Discovery CPMC".

On June 28, 2024, Plaintiff's discovery motions came on for hearing in Department 302 as noticed. The Motion to Compel Discovery CPMC and the Motion to Compel Discovery All were ordered off calendar as not filed and served in compliance with CCP 1005 and the Motion to Compel Discovery was denied without prejudice for failure to file a separate statement in compliance with CRC 3.1345. The Order was signed on June 28, 2024 and filed on July 8, 2024. Plaintiff has not sought relief from or reconsideration of said Order nor attempted to address the defect noted as to the Motion to Compel Discovery. There has been no notice of any discovery motion for hearing in Department 302 on September 6, 2024.

Accordingly, each of these motions is incorrectly calendared and all of them are ordered off calendar.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)
